concerns.text: FR-007 mandates repeating the full analysis on 'warm-dark-matter variant snapshots'. While T029/T031 cover Millennium-II, there is NO task explicitly covering the ingestion or analysis of WDM variants. The 'Data Gap Protocol' in T029 mentions skipping if no URL is found, but no task exists to attempt the fetch or log the specific gap for WDM variants as required by the spec's mandatory cross-validation.

FR-011 mandates 'linear regression of galaxy property ~ shape parameters controlling for halo mass'. T023 implements 'linear regression with mass control', but T021 (mass-matching) and T022 (non-parametric tests) are separate. The tasks do not explicitly link the regression output to the specific 'shape parameters' (axial ratios/triaxiality) defined in FR-001/US-1, risking a regression on binned categories rather than continuous parameters as implied by the spec's 'shape parameters' phrasing.

SC-003 requires measuring 'variance of p-values' across the threshold set and ensuring it is ≤ 0.001. T030 outputs a report with p-values but lacks a specific task to calculate this variance metric and compare it against the 0.001 threshold. Without a task to compute and validate this specific success criterion, the system cannot verify SC-003.

FR-008 requires the 'associational_only=true' flag in ALL output datasets. T026 adds this flag to 'all output datasets' generically. However, T017 (halo_shapes.csv), T025 (statistical_results.csv), T030 (sensitivity_report.csv), T031 (millennium_results.csv), and T038 (alignment_angles.csv) are distinct output tasks. There is no explicit task ensuring the flag is applied to the sensitivity report (T030) or the alignment angles (T038), creating a risk of partial implementation.

T029 references 'WDM variants' in the Data Gap Protocol description but the task implementation only mentions 'Millennium-II data fetcher'. The task description should explicitly state that it attempts to fetch WDM variants as well, or a separate task should be added for WDM ingestion to match FR-007.
